name: Cafe Réveille
location: 610 Long Bridge Street, San Francisco • South of Market
description: Cozy cafe with sidewalk tables and eclectic breakfast fare.
details: Serves American and Levantine-inspired dishes like avocado toast and sandwiches. Great for a relaxed meal or catching up with friends over coffee.

name: The Coffee Movement
location: 1737 Balboa St, San Francisco • Inner Richmond
description: Sleek grab-and-go spot with killer espresso and seasonal sips.
details: Two locations, one near Chinatown and another in Inner Richmond. Limited seating at Balboa St.

name: Atlas Cafe
curatorComment: also hosts a ton of crypto events
description: Charming cafe with patio, perfect for work or casual bites.
details: Offers breakfast, lunch, coffee, and beer/wine. Known for golden lattes and roasted yam sandwiches.

name: Dandelion Chocolate 16th Street Factory
description: Light-drenched industrial-chic cafe with house-made chocolates and pastries
details: Offers informative bean-to-bar chocolate tours.

name: Sextant Coffee Roasters
location: 1415 Folsom St, San Francisco • South of Market
description: Light-filled cafe with exposed brick and beamed ceilings, perfect for getting work done.
details: Spacious interior with a backyard patio.

name: Horsefeather SF
location: 528 Divisadero St, San Francisco • Western Addition
curatorComment: new "afternoon delight" program! open from 2-5pm weekdays for laptops and working
description: Moody tavern with creative cocktails and eclectic New American plates.
details: Open for brunch, dinner, and afternoon work sessions.

name: Kiss of Matcha
location: 750 Clement St, San Francisco • Inner Richmond
description: Matcha haven with soft serve and specialty drinks
details: Cozy spot in a bustling area.

name: Matching Half
location: 1799 McAllister St, San Francisco • Western Addition
description: Cozy neighborhood spot for coffee and light bites, perfect for a sunny day.
details: Offers wifi and outlets, making it ideal for work or study sessions.

name: Le Marais Bakery
location: 498 Sanchez St, San Francisco • Castro/Upper Market
description: Compact French bakery serving authentic pastries and light bites.
details: Feels like a slice of Paris with fresh croissants, crepes, and focaccia sandwiches.

name: Compton’s Coffee House
location: 1910 Fillmore St, San Francisco • Western Addition
curatorComment: it's a small space but really bright and nice vibe!
description: Cozy neighborhood spot for quality coffee and matcha
details: Bright, chill atmosphere perfect for people-watching or a quick work session.

name: Noe Cafe
location: 1299 Sanchez St, San Francisco • Noe Valley
description: Sunny neighborhood spot for coffee and pastries
details: Outdoor seating only, perfect for people-watching.

name: Sightglass Coffee
location: 270 7th St, San Francisco • South of Market
description: Bright, airy coffee haven with on-site roaster and killer espresso
details: Huge two-story space with industrial vibes and plenty of seating.

name: Andytown Coffee Roasters
location: Rooftop, Park Level, 181 Fremont St, San Francisco • Financial District
description: Bright, spacious cafe with park views and plenty of outlets
details: Located on the 7th floor of Salesforce Tower.

name: Linea Caffe
description: Cozy grab-and-go spot for top-notch espresso and local pastries.
details: No indoor seating, but perfect for a quick caffeine fix.

name: La Boulangerie de San Francisco
location: 500 Hayes St, San Francisco • Western Addition
curatorComment: wifi is alright but vibes are out of the roof
description: Chic French-inspired café with tasty pastries and sandwiches.
details: Perfect spot for people-watching on Hayes Street.

name: Ritual Coffee Roasters
description: Artisanal coffeehouse with lighter roasts and signature drinks.
details: Cultivates relationships with growers and roasts beans on-site.

name: Verve Coffee Roasters
location: 2101 Market St, San Francisco • Castro/Upper Market
description: Spacious, sunny cafe with great coffee and food
details: Located in the Castro, it's a popular spot for studying and reading.

name: Vive La Tarte
description: Cozy neighborhood spot with killer croissants and cheesecake.
details: Serves breakfast and lunch.

name: Java Beach Cafe
location: 1396 La Playa St, San Francisco • Outer Sunset
description: Laid-back beachside cafe with solid coffee and casual eats.
details: Outdoor seating lets you soak up the ocean vibes.

name: Simple Pleasures Cafe
location: 3434 Balboa St, San Francisco • Outer Richmond
description: Cozy neighborhood spot for coffee, bagels, and baked goods
details: House-roasted coffee and espresso drinks are the main draw.

name: Flywheel Coffee Roasters
location: 672 Stanyan St, San Francisco • Haight Ashbury
description: Cozy family-owned roaster with exotic brews and chill vibes.
details: Stripped-down industrial setting with a nice backyard.

name: Another Cafe
location: 1191 Pine St, San Francisco • Nob Hill
description: Bi-level coffeehouse with small-batch brews, sandwiches, and free wifi.
details: Perfect spot for remote work or a casual breakfast.

name: Rise & Grind Coffee and Tea
description: Lush, plant-filled coffee oasis near Golden Gate Park.
details: Standout drinks include black sesame matcha and cookie butter lattes.

name: Stable Cafe
description: Charming bi-level café with lush garden patio and greenhouse seating.
details: Perfect for brunch, lunch, or a relaxed work session.

name: The Interval at Long Now
location: Center Building A, Landmark Building A, Fort Mason Center, 2 Marina Blvd, San Francisco • Marina
curatorComment: apparently a community favorite and very lit
description: Futuristic bar with intellectual vibes and creative cocktails
details: Open space with bookshelves and scientific instruments.

name: Stonemill Matcha
description: Minimalist Japanese cafe with stellar matcha and light bites.
details: Known for their black sesame latte and katsu sandwiches.

name: Four Barrel Coffee
description: Sleek Mission cafe with bold coffee and intimate seating.
details: Exposed beams create a rustic ambiance.

name: Hayes Valley Bakeworks
location: 550 Gough St, San Francisco • Downtown/Civic Center
curatorComment: lots of seating/tables for work & chats. this place is also a social enterprise/nonprofit
description: Sleek cafe with housemade bites and Four Barrel coffee in Hayes Valley.
details: Social enterprise supporting a good cause.

name: Caffe Trieste
location: 601 Vallejo St, San Francisco • North Beach
description: Charming old-school Italian café with live music and literary history.
details: Cash only, but affordable prices.

name: The Velvet Raven
location: 155 S Park St A, San Francisco • South of Market
description: Cozy spot for craft beers and comfort food
details: Perfect for a lazy afternoon with coffee and Wi-Fi.

name: Progressive Grounds
location: 400 Cortland Ave, San Francisco • Bernal Heights
description: Moroccan-inspired cafe with vegan options and outdoor seating.
details: Perfect spot for a caramel latte and a leisurely lunch.

name: Cafe International
location: 508 Haight St, San Francisco • Western Addition
description: Funky cafe with live jazz and Turkish coffee
details: Laid-back spot perfect for working or catching Sunday jazz sessions.

name: Müesi Cafe
location: 2055 Union St Suite B, San Francisco • Marina
description: Light, airy cafe with plenty of seating and outlets for post-workout or pre-work hangouts.
details: Perfect spot for getting work done or catching up with friends.

name: NOOK
location: 1500 Hyde St, San Francisco • Nob Hill
description: Cozy corner cafe by day, candlelit wine bar by night
details: Perfect for people-watching or getting work done.

name: Expensify Lounge
location: 88 Kearny Street, 88 Kearny St 16th Floor, San Francisco • Financial District
curatorComment: secret lounge for start up founders with open bar, snacks, and coffee 👀
description: Secret lounge for startup founders with open bar, snacks, and coffee.
details: Stunning views of the city and convenient location.

name: Philz Coffee
location: 2234 Polk St, San Francisco • Russian Hill
description: Laid-back spot for sustainable coffee and health-conscious bites.
details: Spacious interior with ample seating.

name: 1 Hotel San Francisco
location: 8 Mission St, San Francisco • Financial District
curatorComment: great lobby & cafe space for working
description: Waterfront workspace with Bay Bridge views and cozy vibes
details: Hotel lobby doubles as a calm, plant-filled cafe.

name: The Center SF
location: 548 Fillmore St, San Francisco • Western Addition
curatorComment: they only sell tea but the vibes are very zen and lots of space to work. they also do yoga classes.
description: Zen tea house with yoga and open mics
details: Volunteer-run community space offering a variety of teas and cocoa.

name: Hotel Kabuki - JDV by Hyatt
location: 1625 Post St, San Francisco • Western Addition
description: Serene Japanese hotel with a dreamy lobby, koi pond and endless coffee - perfect for posting up with your laptop all day
details: The lobby transforms from a WFH paradise to a sultry lounge scene at night.

name: The Mill
location: 736 Divisadero St, San Francisco • Western Addition
curatorComment: amazing vibe and lots of seating but no wifi :(
description: Bright, airy cafe with top-notch bread and coffee
details: Known for their open-face toasts and Monday night pizzas.

name: Saint Frank Coffee
location: 2340 Polk St, San Francisco • Russian Hill
description: Bright, airy coffee shop with great brews and stylish wood decor.
details: Perfect for casual meet-ups or solo reading sessions.

name: Tenderloin
description: DO NOT VISIT. Most concentrated crime area in SF — 2,890 crimes (678 violent). Rampant drug trafficking, assaults, open drug use, aggressive panhandling day and night.

name: SoMa
description: High risk — 4,567 crimes (567 violent). Frequent car break-ins, smash-and-grab robberies, tent encampments under overpasses. Stay alert south of Market St.

name: Mission District
description: Medium-high risk — 3,890 crimes (456 violent). Mugging and property theft concentrated around 16th St BART; risk increases south of 24th St, especially at night.

name: Bayview
description: DO NOT VISIT. Gang activity and shootings — 2,456 crimes (423 violent). Not a tourist area; locals advise visitors to stay away entirely.

name: Civic Center
description: High risk — part of Central/Downtown with 6,789 crimes (789 violent). Theft-dominant tourist/commercial zone; open drug activity around UN Plaza and City Hall after hours.

name: Union Square
description: High risk — part of Central/Downtown with 6,789 crimes. Tourist-targeted pickpocketing, shoplifting, and car break-ins around the square and nearby parking garages.

name: Fisherman's Wharf
description: Medium risk — Northern district, 3,234 crimes overall. Heavy tourist crowds attract pickpockets; car break-ins frequent in parking areas.

name: Nob Hill
description: Medium risk — Northern district adjacent. Package theft and car break-ins spike along the Tenderloin border; keep valuables hidden.

name: Richmond District
description: Medium risk — Northern district, mixed residential area. Isolated stretches near Golden Gate Park see occasional muggings after dark.

name: Sunset District
description: Medium risk — mostly residential and safe. Car break-ins spike near Ocean Beach parking lots; keep nothing visible in your vehicle.

name: Presidio
safetyHighlights: Large park area with steady daytime recreation traffic.
crimeTypes: Car break-ins at trailhead parking lots
description: Generally one of the safer parts of SF, especially during daytime around trails, museums, and main roads.

name: Presidio Heights
safetyHighlights: Well-lit residential area with consistently lower violent incident rates.
description: Generally considered one of SF's safer residential neighborhoods.

name: Pacific Heights
safetyHighlights: Upscale residential zone with strong daytime foot traffic.
description: Frequently listed among safer SF neighborhoods for walking and staying.

name: Marina District
safetyHighlights: Popular residential and visitor area with frequent street activity.
description: Generally safer for visitors than downtown core zones, with active streets and businesses.

name: Noe Valley
safetyHighlights: Calmer streets and lower violent-crime profile compared with city average.
description: Known as a quieter neighborhood with strong residential character.

name: Glen Park
safetyLabel: Residential pocket with local foot traffic
safetyHighlights: Smaller neighborhood feel and generally lower severe-incident reports.
description: Generally regarded as a safer residential area with neighborhood-scale retail.

name: Diamond Heights
safetyHighlights: Primarily residential with lower violent incident density.
description: Generally calmer than dense nightlife and downtown corridors.

name: Twin Peaks
safetyHighlights: Mostly residential hills with scenic outlook traffic.
description: Generally safer than dense commercial cores, but stay alert at viewpoints.

name: West of Twin Peaks
location: West of Twin Peaks, San Francisco
safetyHighlights: Predominantly residential district with lower violent-crime concentration.
description: Generally safer than entertainment-heavy central districts.

name: Lakeshore
safetyHighlights: Lower violent-crime profile with mostly local residential activity.
crimeTypes: Car break-ins near mall and beach lots
description: Generally a calmer area compared with downtown and nightlife-heavy zones.
